rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical reductive alkylation of buta-1,3-dienes with alkyl halides using a sacrificial iron anode and nickel cathode to deliver trans-olefins with high stereoselectivity
electrochemical_role: Cathodic single-electron reduction of alkyl halide to aliphatic C-centered radical; subsequent cathodic reduction of allyl radical intermediate to allyl carbanion; iron plate serves as sacrificial anode

key_intermediates.name: aliphatic carbon-centered radical (A) from R-X reduction
allyl radical adduct (B) from radical addition to 1,3-diene
allyl carbanion (D) after second cathodic reduction

electron_flow: At the cathode, the alkyl halide undergoes SET to give an aliphatic C-radical (A), which adds to the 1,3-diene to form an allyl radical (B/C); a second cathodic SET reduces this allyl radical to an allyl carbanion (D), which nucleophilically displaces halide from a second R-X (or is protonated) to deliver the trans-olefin, while the Fe anode is oxidized to Fe(II) as the sacrificial counter-process.
rate_determining_step: cathodic SET reduction of the alkyl halide to the carbon-centered radical (Ep = -2.58 V vs SCE for 4-iodotetrahydro-2H-pyran)

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 4-iodotetrahydro-2H-pyran
probe_reagents.observation: Ep = -2.58 V vs SCE, consistent with cathodic reduction generating an aliphatic C-radical

probe_reagents.name: divided-cell experiment
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3a formed in 70% yield in cathode chamber (and 5a in 64%), confirming the productive chemistry occurs at the Ni cathode while Fe anode acts only as sacrificial anode

probe_reagents.name: alkyl halide leaving group variation (I vs Br vs Cl)
probe_reagents.observation: Iodides give highest yields, bromides moderate, chlorides typically 0%, consistent with ease of SET reduction tracking C-X BDE

notes: Phenyl-substituted 1,3-dienes (4) incorporate only a single alkyl group (giving 5) because the benzylic allyl carbanion D is stabilized by the aryl group and is protonated rather than alkylated by a second R-X; unsubstituted buta-1,3-diene incorporates two alkyl groups (giving 3). The trans-selectivity (>20:1) is rationalized by the thermodynamically favored E-allyl carbanion/radical geometry. No TEMPO/BHT radical trapping experiments are reported; mechanism is supported primarily by CV and divided-cell experiments plus literature precedent.
